Ad Text:

You'll play a pivotal role across the full delivery lifecycle-working closely with senior stakeholders (including the COO), coordinating across teams, and ensuring successful delivery of product enhancements, integrations, and data migration.

This role suits someone who thrives in complexity and enjoys wearing multiple hats.

Partner with product teams to deliver platform enhancements and capabilities

Ensure accuracy across loan management processes (payments, schedules, calculations)

Engage senior stakeholders and communicate risks, progress, and outcomes clearly
Contribute to delivery planning and execution across BA, Product, and PM functions
Key Requirements
Extensive experience as a Senior Business Analyst / Functional Consultant in complex transformation programs

Strong background in financial services, lending, or loan management platforms
Experience with data migration validation and financial reconciliation
Solid understanding of system integrations (APIs, vendor coordination, workshops)
Experience in product-led environments with multiple stakeholders

Exposure to private credit or commercial real estate lending
Experience working on the vendor side of transformation programs
Background in smaller or growing businesses
What's on Offer
Competitive salary
12-month fixed term contract
Sydney CBD location (3 days in office)
Opportunity to work on a large-scale transformation program with global impact
High level of stakeholder exposure and ownership

About the Company:
This is an exciting opportunity for a talented Regulatory Reporting Specialist to join a leading multinational brand in the Banking and Financial Services sector. Renowned for its strong market presence and commitment to excellence, this organisation offers a dynamic and inclusive environment where your technical skills and regulatory expertise will be recognised and nurtured.


You'll be joining a high-performing team of 4 professionals who are passionate about precision, compliance, and collaboration. Prepare and lodge accurate and timely regulatory reports in accordance with APRA, and other standards.

CA or CPA qualified with 3-5 years of experience in regulatory reporting or financial control within the Banking or Financial Services industry.

Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with a high attention to detail.
Strong communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively across departments.
Proactive mindset with a continuous improvement approach.


If you're looking to take the next step in your career and make a meaningful impact in a well-established financial institution, this is the perfect opportunity for you.

Job Title: Expression of Interest – Commercial Opportunities at Zinfra (All Levels)
Ad Text:
Commercial Analyst | Contracts Administrator | Senior Contracts Administrator | Commercial Manager | Senior Commercial Manager

Locations: Across the East Coast of Australia

Are you a commercial professional who thrives on solving complex challenges, negotiating smartly, and influencing outcomes across major infrastructure projects?

Do you want to play a meaningful role in Australia’s energy transition?

Zinfra is growing—and we’re opening Expressions of Interest for multiple opportunities in our Commercial team across the east coast of Australia. Whether you're early in your career or already a seasoned commercial leader, we want to hear from you.

About Zinfra

Zinfra is a leading partner in the design, build, operation and maintenance of Australia’s energy and utilities infrastructure. Delivering over a $1B portfolio annually, the work we do is essential to bringing energy to our communities safely and reliably, while powering the nation’s energy transition. With a diverse workforce of more than 2,300, we expertly deliver services across the country.

About the Roles

We have vacancies and upcoming opportunities at all levels, including:

Commercial Analyst
Contracts Administrator
Senior Contracts Administrator
Commercial Manager
Senior Commercial Manager

These roles support either major projects or long‑term operations and maintenance contracts across electricity across our diverse portfolios.

Your Responsibilities Will Include

Depending on the role level, responsibilities may include:

Managing end‑to‑end commercial and contract performance across O&M or capital delivery portfolios
Supporting procurement, contract negotiation, and ongoing commercial governance
Managing contract variations, claims, forecasting, reporting and risk reviews
Partnering with project and operational leaders to drive commercial outcomes
Ensuring contract compliance and supporting effective dispute resolution
Providing commercial insights to improve business performance, cost control, and value creation

About You

To be considered, you will bring:

Experience managing commercial contracts in infrastructure, construction, utilities, or similar sectors
Strong commercial acumen and a collaborative approach to influencing stakeholders
Strong analytical skills and the ability to identify risk and opportunity
A commitment to safety, integrity, and delivering value for customers
Relevant qualifications in business, law, commerce, or engineering (role dependent)

Why You Should Work for Us

What we do matters. Delivering safe and reliable energy makes a difference in people’s lives every single day. We are proud to keep Australia connected while preparing the nation for a renewable future.

Our people are our energy source. At Zinfra, we offer:

Certified family‑friendly workplace benefits
Flexible work arrangements
Generous leave benefits
Ongoing career development and pathways
Wellbeing programme offerings
… and more.

We know Zinfra is a great place to belong—but don’t just take our word for it; hear our team members share what working at Zinfra means to them.  

We champion diversity in life and work experiences. We welcome applications from everyone, including First Nations people, veterans, people living with a disability, and members of the LGBTIQA+ community. If you require adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know.

Recruitment Process

As part of the process, you may be required to undertake pre‑employment checks including a medical assessment, reference checks, and a national police check.

Notice to Third Parties

Zinfra does not accept unsolicited CVs (or any associated fees) from recruitment agencies, search firms or third parties. Interested candidates are encouraged to apply directly.

Interested? Let’s Talk.

For questions or a confidential discussion, reach out:

jack.betton@jemena.com.au 03 9173 8986

Company description:



About VIDA ROADS

VIDA Roads is delivering a massive investment in the state's roads. We're delivering major new road projects across metropolitan Melbourne and regional Victoria while improving the roads people rely on every day.

The West Gate Tunnel Project will give Melbourne a second freeway link between the west and the city - providing a much-needed alternative to the West Gate Bridge and a vital second river crossing.

Northeast Link will fix the missing link in Melbourne's freeway network with new 6.5 km tunnels from Watsonia to Bulleen. We're also overhauling the Eastern Freeway and completing the M80 Ring Road at Greensborough.

Road Programs are also delivering dozens of new roads, bridges, road widening projects and major freeway upgrades.

Come and be a part of our centre of roads excellence.

Job Title: Data Product Engineer (BI & AI Data Products)
Ad Text:
At our core, RSL LifeCare provides care and services to veterans and seniors who live in our communities or are receiving support at home. Our vision of creating proud communities living their best lives is at the heart of everything we do as we continue to build upon our legacy of supporting veterans and their families for more than 100 years.

This is an opportunity for you to join a large social purpose organisation where your skills and compassion will be valued and enable you to make a meaningful difference to people's lives.

We're looking for an experienced Data Product Engineer to help shape how we use data across the organisation.

In this role, you'll design, build, and maintain reliable, reusable data products that support everything from day‑to‑day reporting to advanced analytics and AI.
You'll take the lead on our curated data layer, semantic models, and enterprise KPI definitions-making sure our data is consistent, high‑quality, and easy for the business to use.
Your work will play a key part in getting the organisation ready for AI and helping us scale our analytics capability in a safe and efficient way.
You'll also collaborate closely with the BI Developer, Data Engineer and BI Developer - Insights Partner, working together to build a strong, resilient team and a data environment the whole organisation can rely on.

You will have:

Bachelor's degree in computer science, information systems, data analytics, or related field.
Strong experience in dimensional modelling (Kimball) and enterprise semantic model design.
Advanced Power BI semantic modelling skills, including DAX, KPI/measure design, and performance optimisation.
Hands‑on experience delivering curated analytics datasets using Microsoft Fabric (Warehouse/Lakehouse, Notebooks, Pipelines).
Strong SQL skills and experience implementing business transformation logic.
Experience defining and applying data quality rules for analytics and reporting.
Exposure to supporting self‑service BI through certified datasets and semantic model reuse.
Exposure to preparing datasets for AI and advanced analytics.
Ability to translate business concepts into reusable, well‑governed data products.

Desirable

Certifications in Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, or data analytics platforms.
Strong Python skills for data science use cases.
Experience with data quality monitoring, validation frameworks, or data observability.
Familiarity with data governance (classification, access controls, retention).
Experience with aged care or healthcare data, including regulated environments and clinical or funding datasets.

RSL Life Care offers the following benefits:

Not-for-profit tax benefits - salary packaging of up to $15,900 per year. This allows you to reduce your taxable income by receiving part of your salary as tax-free benefits like super contributions, car leasing, and more.
Staff discounts across 700 select retail stores through Maxxia Rewards
Fitness Passport
Leave Loading - 17.5% pay top up on paid leave

Our commitment to diversity:

We are proud to stand for our unique values: Do the Right Thing, Aim Higher, Care from the Heart, and Act as One team.

We welcome and encourage applications from people across our diverse community, especially from Veterans, current serving ADF personnel, their families and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander applicants. We believe everyone has the right to feel safe, respected, valued and heard regardless of their gender, sexual orientation, ethnicity or disability.


It is a mandatory requirement for a team member working in the Aged Care sector to have a valid Police Check or a valid National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) check, or potentially both, to undertake the inherent requirements of the role. Clearances must be verified prior to commencing employment with RSL LifeCare.

Job Title: Manager, Systems Engineering, and Software Business Analysis
Ad Text:
Cochlear's mission is to help people hear and be heard. As the global leader in implantable hearing solutions, Cochlear is dedicated to helping people with moderate to profound hearing loss experience a world full of sound. We aim to transform the way people understand and treat hearing loss and innovate to connect people with future technologies. Our employees tell us that the number one reason they enjoy working for Cochlear is the opportunity to make a difference to people's lives. Learn and grow with us as we tackle the most complex challenges in helping more people to experience a lifetime of hearing.

The Opportunity

The Manager, Software Systems Engineering & Business Analysis is a senior leadership role responsible for defining and governing system behaviour across Cochlear's complex medical device software ecosystem. You will lead multidisciplinary teams spanning Systems Engineering, Software Business Analysis and System Verification & Validation, ensuring safe, compliant and clinically effective system delivery.

Operating at the intersection of clinical intent, system architecture and regulated delivery, this role provides both people leadership and deep technical authority. You will influence system-level decisions across customer software, workflow engines, algorithmic modules, firmware integration and device communication layers, while building discipline maturity and engineering excellence across the organisation.

Responsibilities include:

Lead and develop multidisciplinary teams across Systems Engineering, Software Business Analysis and System V&V, building capability, engagement and succession

Define and govern system‑level behaviour, ensuring requirements are clear, traceable, verifiable and aligned to clinical intent

Establish and maintain end‑to‑end traceability across user needs, system requirements, interfaces and verification evidence

Lead system‑level technical decision‑making, risk management and issue resolution in complex, safety‑critical environments

Ensure compliance with medical device regulatory and quality standards including ISO 13485, ISO 14971, IEC 62304, IEC 60601 and FDA requirements

Sponsor and chair system‑level design reviews, ensuring architectural alignment, integration readiness and verification depth

Act as a primary interface between architecture functions and delivery teams to ensure effective system implementation

Champion systems thinking, discipline excellence and continuous improvement through guild leadership, process ownership and tool evolution

About You.

As we grow our operations, we are looking for people who share our passion delivering safe, effective, and high-quality hearing solutions. To add value to Cochlear in this role, you'll be able to demonstrate the following skills and experience:

Extensive experience leading multidisciplinary R&D teams in regulated medical device environments

Deep expertise in software‑centric systems engineering, requirements engineering and system‑level verification

Strong working knowledge of medical device regulatory and quality frameworks (ISO 13485, ISO 14971, IEC 62304, IEC 60601, FDA)

Proven ability to define, govern and verify complex system behaviour across software, firmware and hardware interfaces

Excellent decision‑making and risk management capability in ambiguous, safety‑critical contexts

Highly effective communication and stakeholder influence skills across technical, clinical and executive audiences

What Would Make You Stand Out

Experience leading system behaviour definition for workflow‑driven software, parameter‑driven algorithms or real‑time communication systems, along with a track record of building discipline maturity through standards, tooling and engineering process improvement.

Why this role is for you?

This role offers the opportunity to operate as both a people leader and technical authority in one of Cochlear's most complex engineering domains. You'll shape system behaviour that enables safe, reliable clinical outcomes, influence future platform evolution, and build high‑performing teams whose work directly changes lives.

If you feel that you have the skills and experience to be successful in this role and take on new challenges to build your career with Cochlear, please start your application by clicking the apply button below.

How we recognise your contribution

At Cochlear we value and welcome the unique contributions, perspectives, experiences, and backgrounds of our employees and aim to build a culture that celebrates and leverages these differences, creating a sense of belonging and enabling our people to realise their full potential. Through our internal programs and employee benefits, we aim to create an environment where our people will feel value and supported. Whether your focus is on continuous learning, professional development or simply finding an environment which enables you to thrive whilst balancing family or personal life commitments, then we have several programs in place to support you.

For more information about Life at Cochlear, visit www.cochlearcareers.com

Salary: Half a day each Wednesday! Hybrid working.
Ad Text:
Company Description

Fairness feels good 

Make a real impact at AFCA. Where fairness drives every decision. Help us deliver world-class, independent complaints resolution for Australians. As a not-for-profit and progressive financial ombudsman, we’re championing positive change. Achieving our purpose takes progressive thinking, accountability and resilience. At AFCA, our inclusive leadership values every voice. We offer our people flexible work options, thoughtful benefits and opportunities to deepen expertise. Flourish in a diverse, caring culture. Feel the difference of belonging to an organisation intentionally designed to put people first. 

Job Description

AFCA is undertaking a significant expansion of its remit through the Scams External Dispute Resolution (EDR) Project, a critical initiative that will shape how Australians are protected in an increasingly complex financial environment.

We’re looking for a Finance Business Partner to play a pivotal role in designing, influencing and implementing financial change for this landmark program. This is a high-impact opportunity for a commercially astute finance professional who thrives at the intersection of strategy, modelling and stakeholder partnership.

As AFCA’s Finance Business Partner – Scams EDR, you will be a key advisor to senior leaders, working closely with the Scams EDR project team, the broader Finance function, and cross‑functional stakeholders across the organisation.

You’ll lead financial reporting and modelling for the project, support the review and implementation of AFCA’s funding model, and ensure financial integrity as the Scams EDR transitions from design into delivery. This role sits within Corporate Services and reports to the Senior Manager – Finance Business Partnering & Transformation.

What you’ll be doing

In this strategically focused role, you will:

Lead project financial reporting, including grant agreement obligations
Act as the primary point of contact for all Scams EDR finance-related queries
Build and maintain driver‑based financial models covering revenue, costs, capital expenditure and cash flow
Conduct sensitivity analysis and scenario modelling to support executive decision making
Integrate financial model outputs into AFCA’s annual budget processes for the Scams EDR program and operations
Support the review, approval and implementation of changes to AFCA’s funding model in the context of Scams EDR
Represent AFCA in responding to external stakeholder and funding model queries
Collaborate closely with internal stakeholders to develop recommendations and implementation approaches
Manage vendor engagements, systems testing and validation related to funding model and financial system changes
Partner with internal IT, data teams, auditors and third‑party suppliers to ensure robust, end‑to‑end delivery

This is an initial 18-month contract role.

Qualifications

What you’ll bring

You’re a confident finance partner with strong technical capability and the presence to influence at senior levels. You’ll also bring:

Tertiary qualifications in Accounting, Finance, Economics or a related field
CA or CPA qualification
several years’ experience in financial analysis, commercial finance or business partnering roles
Advanced financial modelling capability, including driver‑based models and scenario analysis
Strong systems expertise (e.g. Microsoft D365 FinOps, SAP, Oracle) and advanced Excel skills
Exceptional analytical skills, attention to detail and a high level of numeracy
Clear, persuasive communication skills with the ability to translate complex concepts for diverse audiences
Proven ability to manage competing priorities and deliver under pressure
A collaborative mindset and experience working with cross‑functional stakeholders
High ethical standards and a strong commitment to confidentiality
Additional Information

Additional Information

BOSS Best Places to Work 2024 –credited for its culture, engagement and flexible working arrangements. 
Most Inclusive Workplace 2024 – Australian HR Institute (AHRI) Awards. 
Employer of Choice Public Sector and NFP – Australian HR Awards 2023 
Bronze AWEI Accreditation 2024 – Recognised for LGBTQ+ workplace inclusion. 
Accredited Family Friendly Workplace – Supporting work-life balance and inclusivity. 
Hybrid working – Flexible arrangements with state-of-the-art offices designed for collaboration and wellbeing. 
Inclusive leave options – Flexible public holidays, 20 weeks paid parental leave, gender affirmation leave, women’s health leave, and paid time off over Christmas. 
Locations – A team of over 1,200 dedicated professionals based in modern Melbourne and Sydney CBD offices. 

To apply 

If you’re passionate about fairness and believe your skills align with this role, we encourage you to apply—even if you don’t meet every single criterion. 

We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, cultures, abilities, sexual orientations, and gender identities. If you require any accessibility support during the recruitment process, please reach out to our team at Careers@afca.org.au. 

AFCA is a 2025 Circle Back Initiative Employer - we are committed to responding to every applicant.

A unique opportunity to join a key leading regulator in the energy sector for the Victorian State Government.

Are you a senior portfolio or change leader who thrives at the intersection of strategy, regulation and delivery? Do you bring the confidence to challenge, influence and support executives while maintaining a practical, hands‑on approach?



Your new company

This organisation is a trusted statutory authority responsible for regulating energy safety, supply and efficiency across Victoria. Operating in a complex and highly scrutinised environment, it plays a critical role in ensuring energy is used safely, responsibly and with confidence.

Your new role
Reporting to the Head of Strategy and Risk, the Portfolio Manager plays a pivotal enterprise role in shaping how strategy is mobilised and delivered across the organisation.
This is not an IT or technical portfolio role. It is focused on regulatory, operational and organisational change, providing authoritative oversight of a complex portfolio.

Key responsibilities include:

Providing enterprise‑level stewardship of the portfolio, ensuring the organisation is investing in the right priorities at the right time.
Translating strategic intent into well‑defined, delivery‑ready programs and initiatives.
Supporting regulators and business owners through periods of regulatory and operational change.
Leading portfolio‑level prioritisation, sequencing and trade‑off analysis to balance demand, risk and organisational capacity.
Driving performance, delivery confidence and risk mitigation across the portfolio.
Preparing high‑quality, evidence‑based insights and advice for Executive and governance forums.
Leading and developing a small, diverse team (2 direct reports and potential contractors), fostering inclusive, high‑performance ways of working.
Uplifting portfolio and change capability, frameworks and internal processes across the organisation.
This role requires strong judgement, comfort with ambiguity, and the ability to move seamlessly between strategic advisory and hands‑on leadership.



What you'll need to succeed

Senior experience leading enterprise portfolios or complex change, with credibility advising executives on prioritisation, sequencing and investment trade‑offs.
Proven ability to mobilise strategy, shaping initiatives from concept through to delivery readiness within regulated or complex environments.
Strong executive presence, with sound judgement and the confidence to provide clear, constructive challenge.
Excellent analytical and communication skills, translating complex portfolio, risk and delivery information into actionable insights.
A hands‑on people leader, experienced in leading small, diverse teams and improving performance.
A pragmatic change leadership mindset, supporting leaders through regulatory and operational change.
Sound financial and investment acumen, including business cases, benefits and risk management.
Consulting or advisory background is highly regarded.


What you'll get in return

Permanent ongoing role with a salary of $163,420 - $178,952 + Super
A senior, influential leadership role within a respected Victorian Government regulator.
The opportunity to shape how enterprise change is prioritised, governed and delivered.
Meaningful work that contributes to public safety and regulatory confidence.
Supportive executive leadership, flexibility, and a values‑driven culture.
Ongoing professional growth in a complex, purpose‑led organisation.


What you need to do now

If you're ready to step into a pivotal Portfolio Manager role where strategy, regulation and delivery intersect, click Apply Now or contact parminder.kaur@hays.com.au for a confidential discussion.

If this isn't quite the right role, but you're exploring a senior portfolio or change leadership opportunities, feel free to reach out. And if you know someone who would be a strong fit, please share this opportunity.

With an expanding pipeline of work, we're looking for an exceptional Strategy & Portfolio Manager to join our dynamic, high-performing team and help tackle the most complex and meaningful challenges facing Sime. This is a permanent, fulltime role located in Brisbane.

This role offers strong exposure to executive-level stakeholders and the opportunity to influence strategic direction across the Group and divisions including Industrial, Motors and UMW. You'll contribute directly to corporate and divisional strategy development - shaping strategic choices that support Sime's long-term growth and sustainability.

What you'll do:
In this role, you will work across Group and divisional priorities to:

Support and lead strategic projects that contribute to cohesive Group and Divisional strategy for long-term growth and sustainability.

Frame strategic problems, lead research and analysis, and develop evidence-based options and recommendations that guide investment decisions and priorities.

Partner with senior stakeholders to align priorities and constraints, shape strategic choices, and build commitment through governance and targeted communications.

Build and maintain deep competitor and market understanding, identifying growth opportunities, risks and emerging threats.

Define and agree project scope and outcomes by leading structured information gathering, analysis and stakeholder consultation.

Develop executive-ready communications that distil complex strategic work into clear narratives, choices and trade-offs.

What you'll bring:
We're seeking someone who can operate confidently at Sime level with strong judgement, influence and strategic problem-solving capability. Ideally, you'll have:

Minimum 10 years' experience in senior roles solving complex business problems, shaping direction, and influencing stakeholders to deliver enterprise outcomes.

Demonstrated ability to turn ambiguity into clarity - using structured thinking, analysis and sound judgement to develop options and recommendations.

Strong capability to engage and influence senior stakeholders, with clear, executive-ready communication.

Bachelor's degree (postgraduate desirable).

Why Group Strategy:
If you're looking for breadth, visibility and impact, this is a standout opportunity:

Enterprise-wide exposure: work across Group strategy and divisional strategy (Industrial, Motors, UMW).

Varied, high-impact work: tackle complex, enterprise-wide problems and opportunities that shape Sime's future direction.

Executive engagement and participation in high-impact governance and decision forums.

Join a dynamic team known for high standards, strong thinking, and trusted partnerships across the organisation.

Flexible engagement: available as a permanent appointment or a 2-year secondment (ideal to broaden enterprise experience).

Our Values:
At Sime, we're guided by our values: Act with Integrity, Care for All and Win Together.

*How to express interest
If you meet the above and are looking for a role that will reward and challenge you, please apply now, including a brief note outlining:

a complex strategic problem you've worked on and the approach you took, and an example of influencing senior stakeholders through a clear recommendation and rationale.

Are you a commercially minded finance leader who thrives in fast-paced, operational environments? This is a rare opportunity to step into a high-impact role within one of Australia's most sophisticated retail supply chain and logistics networks, supporting the movement of goods to thousands of locations nationwide. Working at the heart of a complex, high-volume distribution environment, you'll partner closely with operational leaders to drive performance, unlock efficiencies, and influence key strategic decisions that shape the future of the business.

About the Opportunity

Sitting within a large, high-performing finance function, you'll act as a true business partner to distribution centre operations, providing critical insights into financial and operational performance across a national network.

You'll play a key role in translating data into action, helping senior stakeholders understand performance drivers, identify opportunities, and execute initiatives that improve productivity, cost efficiency, and long-term value creation.

Key Responsibilities

Partner with operational and finance stakeholders to drive performance across distribution centre operations
Deliver weekly, monthly and ad hoc reporting on financial and operational KPIs
Provide clear, actionable insights into cost drivers, risks and opportunities
Influence decision-making across areas such as productivity, workforce planning and continuous improvement initiatives
Lead or contribute to national working groups and transformation initiatives
Support budgeting, forecasting and financial planning cycles
Collaborate on major capital projects, including business case development, investment analysis and post-implementation reviews
Engage cross-functionally with teams such as procurement, workforce relations and project delivery to optimise outcomes

What You'll Bring

Ideally 10+ years' post-qualified experience in a commercial finance role
Strong background in business partnering within operational or supply chain environments (distribution, logistics, manufacturing or retail highly regarded)
Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders and drive outcomes
Advanced data analysis and financial modelling capability
Ability to distil complex data into clear, commercially relevant insights
A proactive, curious mindset with a passion for continuous improvement

Why This Role Stands Out

Be part of a large, sophisticated finance function with significant scale and career progression opportunities
Work in a business that sits at the core of Australia's retail and supply chain ecosystem
Gain exposure to major transformation projects and strategic initiatives
Enjoy a flexible working environment with a strong focus on balance and wellbeing
Collaborate with high-calibre leaders across finance, operations and strategy

The Bigger Picture

This organisation plays a critical role in ensuring millions of Australians have access to everyday essentials, every single day. Behind the scenes is a highly complex supply chain operation, and finance is at the centre of enabling it to run smarter, faster and more efficiently.

If you're looking for a role where you can combine commercial thinking with real operational impact, this is an opportunity to step into something genuinely meaningful and career-defining.

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Data Engineer to join our Data and Insights. This role is critical in the ongoing administration, enhancement, and optimisation of our enterprise data platforms. People First Bank (PFB) currently operates on-premise data warehouses and is progressively building a modern Data and Insights capability

About the role 

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Data Engineer to join our Data and Insights team in a 12 months Fixed Term Contract. This role is critical in the ongoing administration, enhancement, and optimisation of our enterprise data platforms. People First Bank (PFB) currently operates on-premise data warehouses and is progressively building a modern Data and Insights capability.

This role is offered as a 12 months Fixed Term Contract with potential permanency or extensions.

Key Responsibilities:

Liaise with business departments to evaluate and determine data needs and requirements. Apply technical and analytical skills to solutions to improve PFB  reporting, advanced analytics, and data insights.

Work with Business Intelligence Analysts, Data Base Administrators, Business Analysts, IT and other key stakeholders to, maintain, organise and improve data warehouse solutions. Assist in the development of future business intelligence strategy and data governance decisions for PFB.

Maintain effective commercial relationships with external service providers to deliver optimal data management and reporting outcomes for the business.

Engineer & model curated Data Warehouse solutions to support BI objectives and timeframes in relation to the development of key analytics and metrics 

Partner with technical teams to build maturity and capability in relation to implementing data capture, ETL, validation, and test methods

Participate in an on-call support roster to help monitor, maintain, and troubleshoot the Data and Insights platform, ensuring high availability and reliability of critical data services.

Maintain and assist in data model documentation, data dictionary, data flow, data mapping and other MDM and Data Governance documentation




About you 

You are the go-to experts in crafting, implementing, and sustaining resilient data processing pipelines. Your role is pivotal in guaranteeing the effectiveness, precision, and scalability of our data infrastructure, impacting not only the Data and Insights team but also extending across the entire organization.

Knowledge and Experience:

Extensive experience in a similar role within a complex data environment.

Strong hands-on experience with ETL tools, preferably SSIS and WhereScape.

Advanced SQL skills, including the ability to write complex queries using sub-queries, nested queries, and other advanced SQL techniques.

Proficient in the Microsoft BI stack, including SSAS, SSRS, and SSIS, with additional exposure to Power BI for reporting and dashboarding.

Experience with CDC (Change Data Capture) tools, specifically Precisely, for real-time and incremental data movement.

Experience working with Azure DevOps, including Git repositories and Azure Pipelines; familiarity with PowerShell scripting and Power BI deployment.

Solid understanding of enterprise data warehouse (EDW) methodologies, including data analysis, data modelling, data integration, and data warehouse design.




Who are we? 

It's an exciting time to join our organisation! On 1 March 2023, two of Australia's most successful member-owned banking organisations merged to form People First Bank – powered by Heritage and People's Choice.  We are Australia's leading mutual banking organisation and a genuine, member-owned alternative to the major banks. 

We exist to create a better future for our members and the communities in which they live. 

We are 100% member owned, with approximately 720,000 members and 1,900 employees. We have dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, and 95 branches across South Australia, Victoria, New South Wales, Queensland and the Northern Territory, trading under two brands, Heritage Bank and People's Choice. 

When you come to work with us, you'll be joining a team of inclusive, friendly, and motivated employees who value making a difference every day for our members, community, and the planet. We are committed to creating an outstanding working environment where you feel supported, can continue to develop and are proud advocates of our members and our business.  




Benefits of working for us  

We believe the little extras can make a big difference in supporting your success. That's why we offer great incentives and benefits including: 

Extensive range of financial and lifestyle benefits, including (but not limited to): 

Paid parental leave and special leave provisions 

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements

Employee banking benefits and discounted insurances 

Career development opportunities, and ongoing training, coaching and support 

A dedicated Employee Assistance Program for you and your family to access in times of need 

Paid volunteering days and access to a diverse range of community and charitable initiatives 




If you are ready to join a supportive team who are passionate about the work they do and the results they achieve, then apply now.

If this sounds like you, then this is the next step in your career. Click 'Apply' now and submit your application, including a cover letter and current resume by 13th April 2026

Job Title: Reporting Manager - Talent Acquisition Reports, Enterprise Account
Ad Text:
Reporting Manager – Enterprise/Financial Services, TA Reports (Sydney)

As the Reporting Manager, you will join a high-performing Hudson Talent Acquisition team, focusing on supporting one of our clients in the Enterprise/Financial Services sector. Your primary responsibility will be to understand and analyze recruitment processes to enhance information management and support strategic decision-making within the TA teams and client Human Resources functions.

What you’ll do:
 
Operate as a dedicated resource for an enterprise RPO account, ensuring reporting and analytics activities align with client priorities, service level agreements, and overall business objectives.
Oversee data governance and ensure compliance with organizational standards across Avature and BI systems.
Lead the design and implementation of reporting strategies and analytics projects from conception to delivery.
Drive innovation by introducing automation, AI, and Power Platform solutions to optimize reporting processes.
Support the creation of budgets in collaboration with senior stakeholders by providing accurate reporting and financial insights to guide resource allocation and strategic decisions.
Provide leadership and mentoring to the reporting team, fostering a culture of continuous improvement.
Collaborate with senior management on strategic initiatives and present insights to inform decision-making.
Manage stakeholder relationships to ensure reporting aligns with business objectives.

What You Will Bring to the Role:
 
Typically requires at least 5 years of experience in reporting or data analysis roles, with RPO experience strongly preferred.
Proficiency in Excel and BI tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau).
Experience with ATS/CRM/HRIS systems (e.g., Workday, SuccessFactors, Avature, Smart Recruiters), with implementation in this space highly sought after.
Strong financial acumen with the ability to interpret and analyze budget data, ensuring alignment between reporting outputs and financial objectives.
Proven leadership and guidance skills, mentoring junior analysts and fostering a culture of continuous improvement.
Champion innovation by driving the adoption of emerging technologies such as AI, automation, and Microsoft Power Platform (including Power Apps) to optimize reporting strategies and enhance team capabilities.
Demonstrated experience working closely with management or leadership teams, including exposure to decision-making processes and strategic-level reporting.
Experience in managing reporting and analytics projects from conception to implementation.
Excellent relationship management skills, with effective communication ability with stakeholders at all levels of the organization.

What Does Great Look Like in This Role:
 
Proactively identifies challenges and opportunities for improvement, driving innovation in reporting methodologies.
Acts as a trusted advisor for reporting and analytics within the business.
Contributes to the budgeting process to enhance profitability.
Fosters team success through mentoring and knowledge sharing.
Receives consistent positive feedback from stakeholders.
Serves as a proud ambassador of Hudson Talent Solutions.

Please note that parts of our communication used in the course of providing our services are generated with AI assistance and have been reviewed for accuracy by a human. As a company, we are committed to the responsible and ethical use of AI in our services.

Are you ready to be part of an organisation that values expertise, passion and diversity? At CFS we know that the foundation of our success lies in our exceptional people. We believe in celebrating individuality, have a passion for high performance and creating an environment where you can unleash your full potential. Our people enable us to make a difference and deliver exceptional experiences to help our customers achieve financial freedom.


Your Team

The Digital Product Owner (Adviser) role sits within our Digital & Customer Experience team alongside other Product Owners, UX/UI Designers and content copy writers..

You will develop and execute our digital strategy and product roadmap for our adviser web portal, Firstnet Adviser. Working closely with Product, Technology, Operations, and Distribution stakeholders, you will be accountable for leveraging adviser insights and research to help shape and prioritise the product backlog to ensure we deliver innovative digital solutions for our advisers.

This role is a 12 Month Maximum term contract in Sydney CBD (Hybrid working).


Your Responsibilities

Lead the end-to-end delivery of the product roadmap for advisers ensuring alignment with business objectives and regulatory requirements.
Conduct market research and user testing to inform product decisions and ensure a seamless user experience.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Product, Distribution, IT, cyber security, and UX/UI designers to define and prioritise project requirements.
Ensure timely delivery of key milestones by working alongside IT delivery and project managers.
Ensure you deliver a seamless user experience that helps drive efficiency for advisers and support staff
Communicate and present regular updates on project progress, risks, and issues to stakeholders including executive leadership team.
Ensure the project adheres to best practices in security and compliance.


Your Capability and Experience

Deep understanding of financial adviser workflows is essential
Understanding of the financial advice industry including emerging business and technology trends
Demonstrated experience as a Product Owner or similar role within the wealth/superannuation industry
Proactive problem-solving abilities, using data and insights to develop strategies, make decisions, and influence senior stakeholders
Demonstrated experience in driving & leading digital initiatives
Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills
Proven track record of delivering outcomes in an Agile environment
Background in delivering adviser or related digital experiences


What to Expect

At CFS, you'll be working among the very best in the wealth management industry. It's an inspiring environment that encourages development and celebrates success. Other things to look forward to:

Access to CFS Employer Super, which offers an expansive investment menu and flexible insurance solutions
Support with financial services, including discounts on home loans and daily bank accounts through our partnership with a leading financial institution
Access to 24/7 confidential support covering safety, medical and mental health services
Additional leave day to celebrate your birthday
Life Leave - 3 days per annum to focus on your wellbeing or celebrate a special event
Exclusive discounts on retail, travel and entertainment


CFS Culture


At CFS we are committed to creating a thriving environment where individuals can flourish. We believe that success is built upon strong teams, and we are dedicated to celebrating uniqueness, championing individuality and supporting a diverse and inclusive workforce. We believe that when you can truly be yourself, you can unlock your full potential.

Apply today and join us in helping Australians to achieve their financial freedom.

Please note, CFS requires all candidates to have full work rights in Australia.

Where we have preferred candidates, background checks (including Police, Employment, Bankruptcy checks, ASIC banned and disqualified persons) will be completed prior to the final preferred candidate's employment being confirmed. The outcomes of the background checks do not preclude the preferred candidate, however, they will be assessed against the inherent requirements of the role.

This role is based on Gadigal Land (Sydney).

We are partnering with a national agribusiness headquartered in Brisbane seeking a Finance Business Partner - Analyst to support group-level performance, revenue analysis and strategic financial planning.

This is a commercially focused role that combines FP&A, management accounting and true business partnering. You will work closely with commercial and operational leaders to provide insight that drives revenue growth, margin performance and long-term sustainability.




The Opportunity

Reporting to the Group Manager FP&A, you will act as a key finance partner to head office stakeholders while supporting group planning, forecasting and performance analysis. The role offers exposure across revenue, pricing, cost control and capital decisions within a fast paced, operationally connected environment.

You will balance structured planning and reporting with forward looking analysis and commercial insight.

Key Responsibilities

Support annual budgeting, monthly forecasting and long range planning
Analyse revenue, pricing, sales mix and gross margin performance
Manage month end activities including accruals, recharges and reconciliations
Provide variance analysis across corporate spend and key cost drivers
Develop financial models and scenario analysis for strategic initiatives
Partner with sales and commercial leaders to support decision making
Support capital investment decisions through business case development
Drive reporting improvements, automation and process enhancement




About You

You are a commercially minded finance professional who enjoys partnering with stakeholders and translating financial data into clear insight. You are comfortable operating across both reporting and forward-looking analysis.

You will bring:

Experience in FP&A, commercial finance or financial analysis
Strong modelling capability and advanced Excel skills
Revenue and margin analysis exposure
Ability to influence and engage non finance stakeholders
CA or CPA qualified or working towards
Proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement




Why This Role?

Broad exposure across revenue, operations and corporate performance
True business partnering with commercial stakeholders
Opportunity to influence planning and investment decisions
National organisation with long term growth focus backed by PE
Collaborative and supportive team environment




You must hold full, unrestricted working rights in Australia.

The Opportunity

We’re partnering with a well-established and leading FMCG group with a strong presence globally. Due to internal movements, they are seeking an experienced Financial Analyst to join the team on 6-month contract. This role suits candidate on a working holiday visa.

Key Responsibilities
Prepare, analyse, and present monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports to support decision-making
Monitor key performance indicators and highlight trends, variances, and business insights
Develop and maintain financial models to evaluate performance and guide strategy

The Director, Valuations provides enterprise-wide leadership, strategic direction and oversight for the valuation function, ensuring the integrity, consistency and appropriate application of valuations across the commercial, retail, industrial and build-to-rent asset classes.

The role sits within the Asset Management team and reports to the General Manager, Strategy and Operations. The Director, Valuations supports owners whilst working closely with the Funds Management and Finance teams.The Director Valuations ensures valuation outcomes support capital allocation, portfolio strategy and long-term value creation, while meeting governance, regulatory and fiduciary obligations. Utilise valuation intelligence to actively support forward looking portfolio insights, alongside the asset management team, that drive long-term risk-adjusted returns.

This role sets the vision, frameworks, operating model and culture for the valuation function, positioning it as a trusted adviser to senior leaders, committees and boards, ensuring valuation insights are leveraged to drive informed, forward-looking decision making across the portfolio.

Your responsibilities will include:

Enterprise Leadership & Culture


Provide visible, values-led leadership to the valuations function, fostering a culture of accountability, independence, commercial acumen and continuous improvement.
Build, develop and mentor senior valuation leaders and specialists, ensuring strong capability, succession depth and alignment to future portfolio needs.
Champion ethical decision-making, professional judgement and valuation independence across all funds and asset classes.

Strategic Valuation Leadership


Set and own the enterprise valuation strategy and frameworks, and standards, ensuring consistency of approach while recognising asset-specific and sector-specific drivers.
Provide strategic insight on valuation trends, market movements and emerging risks, translating technical outcomes into clear executive-level implications.
Act as a trusted adviser on valuation matters across acquisitions, divestments, capital allocation, development feasibility and portfolio strategy

Executive & Stakeholder Engagement


Serve as the primary senior interface with Executive Leadership, Investment Committees, Valuation Committees, Boards, auditors, regulators and key external valuation firms.
Lead complex and sensitive stakeholder discussions on valuation outcomes, judgement calls and market movements, particularly where outcomes are contested or require escalation.
Build and maintain strong, strategic relationships with external valuation partners to ensure quality, independence, consistency and innovation.

Governance, Risk & Compliance


Maintain ultimate accountability for compliance with fund valuation policies, regulatory standards and governance frameworks.
Oversee and lead the preparation, sign-off and presentation of Valuation Committee and Board papers, ensuring valuation movements, drivers and implications are clearly articulated.
Sponsor periodic reviews of valuation policies, methodologies and controls to ensure they remain fit for purpose in evolving market and regulatory environments.
Maintain oversight of valuation model risk, key assumptions, sensitivities, and scenario analysis, ensuring transparency, documentation, and auditability.

Portfolio Insight & Value Creation


Oversee valuation outcomes across the full portfolio, ensuring consistency while enabling informed decision-making at asset, fund and enterprise level.
Partner with Asset and Fund teams to identify value-enhancing initiatives, capital optimisation opportunities and risk mitigations informed by valuation insights.
Ensure valuation data and analysis are effectively leveraged to support performance assessment, strategy formulation and capital decisions.

Operating Model & Continuous Improvement


Design and oversee the valuation operating model, balancing rigour, efficiency and responsiveness to business needs.
Drive continuous improvement in valuation processes, data quality, systems and analytics to enhance insight, scalability and decision-making.
Explore and promote innovation and technology in valuation approaches, modelling techniques and market intelligence, including being across emerging technology, its potential application and value
Be well informed on emerging technologies and how they can be ultilised to streamline processes.

Experience


15+ years' senior experience across property valuation, funds management, investment or related roles, with deep exposure to complex, institutional portfolios.
Proven experience leading senior professionals and enterprise-level functions.
Experience working with landlords and owners to deliver valuations outcomes
Demonstrated ability to influence Executive, Committee and Board-level stakeholders.

Qualifications


Tertiary qualifications in property, finance, economics or a related discipline.
Professional valuation credentials required.
Finance or investment credentials highly regarded.

Leadership Attributes


Strategic, commercially astute leader with the ability to balance independence with partnership.
Highly credible communicator able to translate complex valuation concepts into clear, actionable business insights.
Confident decision-maker who operates effectively in ambiguous and high-stakes environments.
Values-driven leader who demonstrates integrity, curiosity, accountability and collaboration.

All of these attributes are preferred, if you think you may be the right fit for the role please still apply.

Excellent written and verbal communication, with an ability to influence decision‑making.

Ad Text:
Our Why 

Datacom works with organisations and communities across Australia and New Zealand to make a difference in people’s lives and help organisations use the power of tech to innovate and grow.

About the Role (Your Why) 

At Datacom, we connect people and technology to solve challenges, create opportunities, and unlock new possibilities for the communities we serve. Our mission is simple: make a meaningful difference through the power of innovation. We partner with organisations and communities across Australia and New Zealand to improve lives and help businesses use technology to innovate, grow, and succeed.

As a Conversational AI & CX leader you will blend hands-on technical expertise with commercial acumen and the ability to engage deeply with customers at every level. You’ll design, build, showcase and optimise cutting-edge conversational solutions that inspire and deliver measurable business value, driving improved customer and employee experiences.

If you’re passionate about customers and obsessed with designing human-centric experiences that deliver ROI, this is your stage. You see the bigger picture, yet dive into the details, and you’re recognised as a thought leader and practitioner in the CX and EX space.

About the team

You’ll be part of our dynamic, fast-growing Conversational AI team across ANZ and Asia, collaborating with some of the most innovative and recognisable brands in Australia and New Zealand. In this pivotal role, you’ll help shape next-generation service experiences by applying Conversational AI and broader AI capabilities to transform customer engagement. Your ability to navigate ambiguity and guide teams through technical and organisational complexity will be essential to driving impactful outcomes across a broad range of industries.

We value flexibility – our offices are based in Adelaide, Brisbane, Canberra, Melbourne, Perth, Sydney, – and we support working from home too. We empower you and your team to balance collaboration and work-life harmony. If you’re an innovative thinker who thrives at the intersection of technology and customer outcomes, you’ll inspire clients with visionary ideas while delivering tangible, high-quality solutions. Your collaborative approach, technical credibility, and passion for CX/EX will make you a trusted advisor and a driver of change.

What You’ll Do

We work in an agile environment where no two days are the same – responding to evolving customer, technology, and business needs. You will:

Create compelling, customer-focused experiences powered by Conversational AI technologies and be comfortable presenting and discussing these with customers and prospects
Ideate, design, build, and deliver powerful demonstrations that showcase CAI capabilities to existing and prospective clients
Apply advanced conversational design principles to craft intuitive, human-centric interaction flows
Transition seamlessly between deep technical conversations with engineers and strategic discussions with business leaders
Collaborate with customers to shape their CX and EX visions – inspiring new ideas and provoking thoughts about the ‘art of the possible’
Identify automation opportunities in contact centre and retail environments that deliver ROI while enhancing frontline operations
Manage and optimise delivery of current solutions including intent management, conversational structure and integration with customers' digital environments
Work with customers to identify tuning and refining opportunities to increase bot containment and improve agent and customer satisfaction within risk tolerance and accessibility standards
Work effectively across stakeholders to translate ambitious customer visions into practical, scalable solutions
Apply change management methodologies and agile delivery techniques to ensure seamless adoption and lasting impact
Plan and integrate next‑generation technologies into the architectural strategy and build strategic relationships with software and hardware vendors to inform roadmaps and GTM approaches

What you’ll bring

Required Experience

Proven (3+ years) expertise in Conversational AI and CX strategy delivering measurable improvements
Strong understanding of automation in contact centre and retail environments
Ability to lead and contribute to project management and implementation activities
Exceptional stakeholder engagement and communication skills, able to navigate technical and business conversations
Experience with platforms such as Twilio, Cognigy, Genesys, and Microsoft
Proven leadership experience and excellent presentation and stakeholder management skills
Experience in solution and design services, scoping, configuration assistance and needs assessments

Nice to Have

Strong working knowledge of change management and agile delivery frameworks
Background in outsourcing or working with systems integrators
Experience with Prosci Change Management methodology
Familiarity with ITIL, application management and integration into core business systems
Understanding of key cloud vendors such as AWS, Azure, GCP and commercial awareness of vendor architectural strategies

Why join us here at Datacom?

Datacom is one of Australia and New Zealand’s largest suppliers of Information Technology professional services, while maintaining a dynamic, agile, small-business feel. It's our people that give Datacom its unique culture and energy.

At Datacom you'll be recognised and valued for your contributions. We're growing year on year and can provide stability, career opportunity and a collegial, agile, flat-structured environment that empowers people and promotes autonomy. We care about our people and provide a range of perks such as social events, chill-out spaces, remote working, flexi-hours and professional development courses to name a few. We operate at the leading edge of technology to help ANZ’s largest enterprise organisations explore possibilities and solve their greatest challenges, so you will never run out of interesting new challenges and opportunities.

If you would like to know more and this sounds like you. Please apply online today!

What We Offer

Strong customer-facing role with real influence on delivery outcomes

Supportive team environment with room to grow

Competitive salary based on experience

Job Title: Domain Solution Architect – Mining, Oil & Gas
Ad Text:

Partner with delivery leadership to ensure quality, margin protection, and client satisfaction.

Contribute to Mining, Oil & Gas‑specific offerings, accelerators, and go‑to‑market strategies.

Support capability development and mentoring across analytics and solution architecture teams.

KPIs & Success Measures

Revenue, pipeline, total contract value and margin attainment.

Bid win rate and opportunity conversion.

Solution quality measured by delivery predictability and reduced rework or escalations.

Client satisfaction, executive sponsorship, and repeat business.

Contribution to reusable assets, offerings, and practice capability uplift.

Margin protection and delivery governance effectiveness.

Maintain 40% utilisation on Data & AI project delivery.

Skills & Experience

Industry & Domain Leadership

15+ years’ experience leading enterprise data and digital initiatives across Mining, Oil & Gas and other asset intensive sectors operating models and safety‑critical environments.

Proven track record advising executives on data strategy, operating models, governance, compliance, and risk to drive measurable business outcomes.

Enterprise Data Strategy & Governance

Expertise in Data Governance & Security, Data Maturity Assessments, Strategic Lifecycle Management, and Enterprise Information/Data Modelling (Kimball, Inmon, Data Vault, Enterprise Information Model).

Hands‑on leadership with DMBOK/DAMA and TOGAF aligned practices; implementation of metadata management, lineage, quality, and master/reference data disciplines.

Architecture & Modern Platforms

End‑to‑end solution design across modern data platforms, including lakehouse and data fabric patterns; event‑driven and streaming architectures; microservices integration and DevOps enablement.

Advanced experience with Azure (Azure SQL, Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Factory/ADF, Cosmos DB), AWS, Snowflake, Informatica and google data ecosystems.

Systems & Tools

Databricks (Delta Lake, ML pipelines) and Microsoft Fabric (e.g., OneLake, lakehouse, pipelines, semantic models).

Azure Synapse, Snowflake, Confluent (Kafka), MuleSoft, Salesforce, SAP, DBT, SSIS, ADF, PowerShell, Git, Jira/Confluence.

BI & Analytics: Power BI, Tableau, SSRS; advanced Python/R analytics workflows and enablement of data science platforms.

Data Engineering & Integration

Design and leadership of robust ingestion, transformation, and inter‑system interoperability across ERP/CRM and OT/IT boundaries.

Implementation of reusable data migration engines and reconciliation/exception reporting at scale.

Presales, Commercials & Delivery Governance

Executive presales leadership (discovery, solution options, estimation, assumption & risk registers), RFP/RFT authorship, solution governance, and handover into delivery with clear NFRs and transition plans.

Proven margin protection through architecture guardrails, risk mitigation, quality gates, and measurable delivery outcomes.

Leadership & Practice Building

Practice uplift (capability frameworks, accelerators, reusable IP, and cross‑functional collaboration with product, security, finance, and operations.

About ElectraNet

ElectraNet builds, owns, operates and maintains the high voltage electricity transmission network which delivers the energy South Australians rely on to power our homes, businesses and communities. Our people describe our culture as supportive, inclusive and community‑oriented, where we genuinely want each other to succeed.

As an industry leader, we are at the forefront of South Australia's clean energy transition and are committed to strengthening how work flows across the organisation to support sustainable performance.

Employee Benefits

ElectraNet is committed to empowering our people and recognising their contribution with a competitive remuneration package of $146,000 + 12% superannuation + bonus (up to 15% of package). In addition, we are proud to offer:

5 weeks annual leave per year
15 days personal/carers leave per year (plus an additional 5 days on first day upon starting with the organisation)
16 weeks paid parental leave
Additional paid volunteering and office closure leave
Flexibility to utilise public holiday leave entitlements for other cultural celebrations
Employee Assistance Program for your personal and professional wellbeing

Visit our careers page to read more about our employee benefits.

The Opportunity

Reporting to the Head of Business Improvement, the Process Excellence Lead helps ElectraNet improve how work gets done by designing simpler, more efficient and customer‑focused end‑to‑end processes.

Working closely with leaders and cross‑functional teams, the role applies lean thinking, service design and data‑driven analysis to understand current‑state processes, identify inefficiencies and design practical, sustainable improvements.

This is a hands‑on process design role. The Process Excellence Lead focuses on doing the deep process work — translating business needs into tangible artefacts and supporting teams to implement improved ways of working.

Key aspects of the role include:

Mapping complex end‑to‑end processes using customer journey mapping, service blueprinting and value stream mapping
Designing future‑state processes that improve flow, reduce waste and enhance customer and employee experience
Analysing cycle times, hand‑offs, pain points and inefficiencies using qualitative and quantitative data
Translating insights into clear artefacts such as journey maps, service blueprints, process documentation and requirements
Facilitating workshops and working sessions to align stakeholders and co‑design solutions
Measuring success through improved flow, simplified processes and measurable operational outcomes
About You

This role suits a process specialist who enjoys getting into the detail and improving how work actually gets done.

You may come from a background such as business analysis, process excellence, lean operations, service design or operational transformation, and have demonstrated experience designing and improving complex processes across multiple stakeholder groups.

We're looking for someone who:

Has strong experience in customer journey mapping and /or service blueprinting, including personas and multiple perspectives (e.g. customer, employee, technology, leaders)
Is comfortable working with complex, cross‑functional processes and identifying waste, hand‑offs and bottlenecks
Applies lean principles pragmatically to improve flow and efficiency
Is curious, patient and resilient, and consistently challenges legacy ways of working to rethink processes
Can facilitate sessions and influence stakeholders to think differently about processes
Enjoys translating ambiguity into clear, usable artefacts
How To Apply

Please apply online via our careers page before 22 April 2026. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as soon as possible, as interviews and appointments may occur prior to the closing date. Applicants can also register for job alerts via our careers page. Position Descriptions can be obtained by clicking here.

Successful applicant will be required to undergo background checks. These checks may include global criminal history checks, reference checks, and validation of qualifications, employment history, Australian work rights, and any additional checks depending on the role.

ElectraNet is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities, and lived experiences—including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disabilities, neurodivergent individuals, and those from culturally and linguistically diverse communities.

We are proud to be a Work180 Endorsed Employer. You can read more about our key benefits, including flexible work options, leave and other supports ﻿on our website.

About the ROLE: Head of Data & AI

Location: Sydney

Permanent Full-time Role

The Head of Data & AI (Artificial Intelligence) will shape, build, and enable the delivery of nxzen’s Data and Artificial Intelligence proposition, with a primary focus on leveraging AI for Asset Intelligence and Operational Excellence. The role targets Utilities in Australia generally, with a particular emphasis on Australian Electricity Distribution Networks.

This role reports to the Digital CoE (Centre of Excellence) Head and collaborates closely with colleagues in the wider nxzen group. Including but not limited to other CoE teams, Client Partners, other group companies, etc. The role is both market-facing and works internally to build our capabilities in applying Artificial Intelligence to drive smarter asset management, predictive analytics, Operational Excellence, and performance improvement across utilities in Australia, with a particular focus on electricity distribution networks.

Key responsibilities for the role include: 

Strategy & Growth

Define and execute the Data & AI (Artificial Intelligence) strategy for Asset Intelligence and Operational Excellence, aligned to nxzen's business objectives and the needs of Australian utility clients.

Identify and prioritise opportunities to expand nxzen's footprint across Australian Electricity Distribution Networks and the broader utilities sector.

Work with Client Partners, clients, and NNNCo to develop go-to-market solutions and bids that combine AI-driven analytics, IoT, and asset performance improvement.

Develop compelling value propositions that demonstrate how Artificial Intelligence can enhance asset intelligence, operational efficiency, and network resilience for electricity distribution businesses.

Client & Stakeholder Engagement

Act as a senior point of contact for key utility clients, particularly Australian Electricity Distribution Networks, building trusted relationships with operations, digital, and engineering leaders.

Support Client Partners and Delivery Directors in shaping client transformation journeys that leverage AI for asset intelligence and operational excellence outcomes.

Represent nxzen externally at industry events, contributing to thought leadership and brand positioning in the Australian utilities and energy sector.

Service & Solution Development

Shape the Data & AI product and service roadmap, ensuring solutions are technically robust, scalable, and commercially competitive for the Australian electricity distribution market.

Drive the development of AI-powered solutions for asset health monitoring, predictive maintenance, risk assessment, network optimisation, and operational decision-making.

Maintain awareness of emerging technologies and trends (AI/ML, generative AI, IoT, 5G, edge computing, OT security, digital twins) and translate these into practical solutions for utility clients.

Programme Delivery Oversight

Oversee successful delivery of Data & AI programmes for utility clients, ensuring outcomes are met on quality, cost, and schedule.

Implement strong governance, assurance, and performance metrics across projects, with particular attention to the regulatory and compliance environment of Australian electricity distribution.

Ensure operational compliance with security, safety, and regulatory requirements applicable to Australian utility networks.

About YOU

Essential Knowledge

Extensive experience in Data & Artificial Intelligence, with a demonstrated ability to apply AI/ML techniques to asset intelligence and operational excellence in utility or infrastructure environments.

Deep understanding of Australian Electricity Distribution Networks, including regulatory frameworks (AER), network management challenges, and asset lifecycle management.

Proven ability to operate across IT, OT, and ET environments, integrating diverse systems to drive digital transformation and enhance asset performance using AI.

Hands-on experience managing, analysing, and deriving insights from IoT devices, OT systems, digital twins, simulation platforms, and asset health models (e.g., NARMS, CNAIM).

Demonstrated track record of leveraging AI and integrated data to inform asset performance improvement, risk assessment, and strategic decision-making.

Extensive experience applying IEC CIM, ISO 14224, and Ops 4.0 frameworks to support AI-driven asset intelligence and operational excellence initiatives.

Skilled in collaborating with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to design and deliver technically robust, scalable, and commercially viable AI-powered asset intelligence solutions.

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Computer Science, Data Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline.

Desirable Experience

Experience selling or delivering AI and data solutions to Australian electricity distribution businesses (e.g., Ausgrid, Endeavour Energy, Essential Energy, AusNet, Powercor, SA Power Networks, Energex, Ergon Energy, Western Power, or similar).

Familiarity with Australian energy market structures, the National Electricity Rules, and the regulatory investment test (RIT-D) processes.

Experience with AI/ML platforms and tools commonly used in utility asset management and operational analytics.

Track record of building and leading high-performing teams in a consulting or professional services environment.

Professional certifications in Data & AI, IoT, or Analytics (e.g., Certified Data Scientist, AI/ML certifications, or Analytics Professional certifications).

ISO 55000 / Asset Management certification.

IEC CIM or related utility data modelling standards.

OT / Cybersecurity certifications (e.g., CISSP, CISA, or relevant OT security training).

 How to APPLY

To apply, please submit a copy of your updated resume. Screening will be conducted as we receive applications, and ONLY shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

For more information or to have a confidential discussion, please contact our People & Culture Manager, Shreya Pahwa on shreya.pahwa@nxzen.com.
