This role is at the heart of our "People First" culture, focusing on coaching, engagement, and the strategic optimization of BA resources to meet delivery demand.

Key responsibilities include:  

·        Lead end-to-end business analysis across the project lifecycle – Elicit, analyse and document requirements; process and related artefacts; user stories, use cases and storyboards; and support design, build, test and deployment to ensure solutions truly meet business needs. Champion legal and compliance obligations requirements applicable, where required engage with business owner to ensure compliance is met. 

·        Migration & Transformation Excellence: Direct the analysis of source-to-target data and process gaps. You will be responsible for identifying and documenting transitional requirements to ensure a seamless "Cutover" from legacy systems to our new banking platform.

·        People Leadership & Engagement – Actively develop direct reports through coaching and performance management. You will drive employee engagement scores by fostering a supportive BA community and role-modelling our bank’s values.

Strategic Delivery & Value Alignment – Drive the delivery of critical initiatives according to program roadmaps. Partner with Program Owners and Tech Leads to ensure BA activities maximise customer value and align with strategic objectives.

Stakeholder & Risk Management – Strengthen alignment between Chapter and Program stakeholders. Own and manage risk within appetite, ensuring controls support business objectives and coaching teams to uplift risk behaviours.

Capacity & Resource Optimisation – Take ownership of accurate resource forecasting across the program. Optimise resource allocation and ensure efficient use of team capability to meet fluctuating delivery demands.

Capability Uplift & Continuous Improvement – Embed BA chapter standards and tooling. Lead the adoption of new ways of working and drive value-driven improvements that raise overall program performance and professional development standards.

 About you 

This is more than a traditional lead role; it is a leadership position that requires a strategic mindset and a passion for developing people. You are a "People Leader" first, dedicated to shaping a high-performing culture while maintaining the technical excellence required to navigate complex banking transformations.


 To be successful in the role, you must have the following qualifications or skills:

Proven Leadership Experience – Demonstrated ability to lead, recruit, and mentor BA teams, with a track record of uplifting capabilities and fostering high engagement.

Proven Transformation Experience: A track record of success in large-scale transformation programs, specifically in mapping complex data/process gaps and managing the nuances of transitional states.

Expert Analytical Skills (10+ years) – A seasoned BA with 8+ experience in all facets of business analysis.  Deep experience in Finance or complex IT environments, with mastery of requirements elicitation and delivery across both Agile and Waterfall methodologies.

Strategic Stakeholder Management – Exceptional ability to bridge business and technology, building trusted relationships with Senior Leaders and Program Owners.

Risk & Financial Acumen – Experience embedding risk culture and managing resource forecasts to optimise team utilisation within a program.

Growth Mindset – A proactive approach to professional development and a "Resourceful & Optimising" mindset.

Financial services industry experience

Customer centric product development 

Process analysis and documentation 

Data management/mapping; Technical interface specification development 

Adaptable and capable in dealing with complexities and ambiguity in a constantly evolving environment. 

Experience managing multiple activities and issues across numerous projects in a high paced environment. 

Desirable skills for the role:

Customer centric product development in Banking eco-systems and integration

Process analysis and documentation 

Technical interface specification development 

Proficiency in SQL for data querying, analysis, and reporting 

Experience in digital API source-to-target mapping for data integration and transformation.  

Experience working within a customer-focused team in the Payments, Cards and Fraud environment. 

Qualified Professional – Degree qualified with ECBA (or higher) IIBA, Lean Six Sigma certification and proficiency in JIRA, Confluence, and Visio.

Strong Agile methodology certifications/experience (Scrum, SAFe, etc) and proactive promoter of lean, agile best practices  

 Location

Whilst our primary office locations are across Adelaide, Brisbane and Toowoomba, we are pleased to be able to consider applications from employees within Australia for this role. We also encourage you to put thought into how the work may be undertaken given your location and what support you may require.

Why you’ll love working with us: 

People First Bank – our new brand – says exactly what we’re all about: people. We're committed to supporting and growing our team and creating an outstanding work environment.

You’ll have access to financial and lifestyle benefits to support your success and individuality:

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements.

Enhance your salary with discounts on banking, health insurance, and shopping across Australia.

Advance your career with training, study support, and project opportunities.

Competitive leave benefits that empower employees to take time for themselves and loved ones.

Wellbeing support with our Employee Assistance Program, wellbeing leave, mental health app, and mental health ambassadors’ program.

Make a difference with paid volunteering, Workplace Giving, and diverse community initiatives.

Be recognised for your contributions through our peer-driven recognition program.

 A bit about People First Bank:  

In March 2023, People’s Choice and Heritage Bank merged to form Heritage and People’s Choice Limited - Australia's leading mutual bank.  We are now on an exciting journey to bring together our products, services, and technology under a single brand - People First Bank.

We have over 700,000 members and 1,900 employees, with dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, an Australian-based contract centre and branches across five states. 

With a vision ‘To deliver positive change through banking’, People First Bank serves members and contributes to the community, while offering an inclusive, flexible work environment. We’re big enough to make an impact; but agile enough to influence and do interesting work.

About the Role
An opportunity has arisen for an experienced PMO Analyst to join a large-scale regulatory remediation program within a leading Australian bank. This program forms part of a broader regulatory commitment and enforceable undertaking and is focused on strengthening how sensitive customer situations are managed, including hardship, bereavement, collections and complaints.

The program is now entering a critical phase with a newly established leadership team and significant investment to stabilise and accelerate delivery. The PMO Analyst will play a key role in supporting program governance, reporting and delivery coordination across multiple work streams, helping to bring structure, visibility and control to a complex, enterprise-wide initiative.

The program consists of several key streams including Bereavement, Deceased Estates, Hardship, Collections, Complaints Management, Customer Event Management and Customer Remediation, all contributing to a high-profile transformation running through to 2027.

Key Responsibilities

Support PMO capability across a complex regulatory remediation program within retail banking
Coordinate governance, reporting and delivery activities across multiple program work streams
Consolidate RAID logs, status reports and delivery updates into a single program-level view
Track interdependencies, milestones and critical paths across streams
Support SteerCo, governance forums and regulatory reporting requirements
Maintain consistency in PMO standards, templates and reporting cadence
Provide oversight across risks, issues, dependencies and delivery progress
Support program financials, resourcing and forecasting activities
Work closely with stream leads, project managers and senior stakeholders to enable effective delivery

About You

Proven experience operating as a PMO Analyst within large-scale, multi-stream programs
Background in banking or financial services, ideally within regulatory or remediation environments
Strong understanding of governance, reporting, risk and issue management
Experience consolidating multiple work streams into a clear program-level view
High attention to detail with the ability to manage complexity and competing priorities
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
Experience supporting program uplift or recovery environments will be highly regarded
This is a high-impact opportunity to play a key role in stabilising and delivering a critical regulatory program within a leading banking organisation.

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply.

Collaborate with Data Analysts, Administrators, GIS Analysts, and business SMEs to expand platform capabilities and meet client needs.

Job Title: Senior Technical Business Analyst - Data and Analytics
Ad Text:
Company description:



About Capgemini:

Capgemini is a diverse collective of more than 350,000 strategic and technological experts based across more than 50 countries, partnering with world-renowned clients to transform and manage their businesses.

We are dedicated to leveraging cloud, data, AI, connectivity, software, digital engineering, and platforms to address the entire breadth of their business needs. This passion drives a powerful commitment - to unlock the true value of technology. ​

Our business in Australia and New Zealand has over 3,000 team members devoted to helping clients get the future they want.

Now is the time to join our rapidly growing team who are at the forefront of finding new ways technology can help us reimagine what's possible, collecting unique career experiences with global brands and game-changing tech projects.



Job description:

Job Description

About the Service Line:

Capgemini Global Insights & Data business line is a market leader in Data Engineering, Cloud Data Platforms, Data Science, and AI and Advanced Analytics across all sectors including financial services, public sector, consumer products, telecommunication & energy resources. Our offerings include end-2-end data integration to cloud platforms, complete suite of data engineering capabilities and a category of sector-based advanced analytics and AI driven solutions.

This is a unique opportunity within the Insights & Data (I&D) Global Business Line (GBL) to help grow the data, AI and Data Science engagements across all sectors in Australia and New Zealand. You will help shape and deliver complex projects and programs of work for our clients. You will understand our client's need to bring maximum value, impact, and innovation to the market as you oversee the delivery of client engagements for data and AI initiatives.



Let's talk about the roles and responsibilities:

The technical business analyst will work closely with business and technical stakeholders to elicit, analyse, validate and document business and technical requirements across data and analytics initiatives. This includes requirements related to data ingestion, transformation, modelling, reporting and analytics use cases.
Translate business needs into clear, structured artefacts such as requirements specifications, user stories, acceptance criteria, process models, data mappings and solution designs.
Act as a key bridge between business stakeholders and delivery teams including data engineers, analytics engineers, architects and reporting specialists.
Facilitate and lead workshops, interviews and design sessions to drive shared understanding, clarify scope, and support solution alignment.
Produce high‑quality written documentation and presentations suitable for both technical and non‑technical audiences.
Build strong, trusted relationships with stakeholders through effective communication, expectation management and proactive engagement.
Work within Agile and/or hybrid delivery environments, participating in ceremonies such as backlog refinement, sprint planning and showcases.
Experience and Qualifications:
Demonstrated experience as a Business Analyst / Technical Business Analyst, with a solid foundation in core business analysis practices and techniques.
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to communicate effectively across business and technical audiences.
Proven experience facilitating workshops, requirement‑gathering sessions and collaborative design activities.
Experience delivering Data and Analytics projects, including data platforms, reporting, dashboards or analytics solutions.
Understanding of SQL and relational data concepts, with the ability to work with data engineers and analysts on data‑driven requirements.
Familiarity with data reporting and visualisation tools and concepts (e.g. enterprise reporting, dashboards, KPIs).
Strongly preferred: Exposure to modern cloud data platform technologies (e.g. Azure, AWS, GCP, lakehouse or data warehouse platforms).
Experience working in Agile delivery environments (e.g. Scrum, Kanban) is desirable.
What You'll Love About Working Here:
Exposure to transformational programs in AI & Data portfolio.
Career growth through learning platforms, certifications, and global mobility.
Inclusive culture backed by our "Inclusive Future for All" commitment.
Competitive total rewards and recognition programs.

Job Title: Senior Data Scientist (Fraud Risk or Credit Risk Modelling)
Ad Text:
Optus is an Australian telecommunications company, delivering more than 11 million services to our customers every day across mobile, broadband and digital solutions.



The Billing and Credit function, part of Group Finance and Corporate, oversees billing, payments, collections, recoveries, credit, and fraud risk management across Optus. As a Senior Data Scientist within this team, you will leverage advanced analytics and machine learning to deliver timely, accurate, and actionable insights that help identify emerging risks, strengthen fraud and credit controls, and reduce financial loss—ensuring a seamless billing and payments experience for Optus customers.

This role requires a strong mix of technical expertise and business acumen, applying tools such as Databricks, SQL, Python, or R to design, deploy, and refine predictive models and scorecards. You will extract and analyse large datasets, develop innovative risk solutions, and present insights through compelling visualizations to guide decision-making. With experience in credit, fraud, and collections, you'll play a critical role in shaping strategies that protect revenue, manage risk, and support sustainable growth across Consumer, Small Business, Mid-Market, Enterprise, and Wholesale segments.

What You'll Do

Lead delivery of advanced data models and analytics driving all Credit and Fraud Risk decisions across Optus Group.
Design and implement strategy changes to manage 3M+ applications annually through automated decisioning within SLA.
Oversee backend system development and reporting tools (Tableau, Cognos, IDW/EDW, Credit systems, SAS/R models).
Partner with senior stakeholders and business units to enhance financial performance and implement key initiatives.
Plan, track, and coordinate performance targets, transformation priorities, and strategic reviews across Group Credit & Finance.

What makes you perfect for the role?

BA/BS degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, Analytics, or equivalent applied quantitative field
Advanced data analysis, SQL, Python, R, Databricks; strong financial and predictive modelling; ML algorithms applied to business problems.
5+ years in data science/finance roles with telco expertise; credit, fraud, and collections risk management; commercial and regulatory understanding.
Proven ability to build business cases, conduct financial modelling, and translate complex data into actionable recommendations.
Skilled at influencing and presenting to senior management; experience leading initiatives across Product, Sales, Commercial, and Corporate functions.
Strong presentation, negotiation, and interpersonal skills; ability to manage diverse stakeholders and drive cross-functional projects.

What's in it for you?

Competitive remuneration and employee discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and unique shopping discounts with our retail partners.
Flexible working arrangements with opportunities to work three days in the office, two days remote or home.Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.
We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.
Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.
Build meaningful connections through employee-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).
Put your wellbeing first with free access to counselling and support services, 24/7 — in-person, by phone, SMS, or video.

At Optus, we are strengthened by others and that means valuing diversity and saying ‘yes' to embracing individual differences. We are committed to ensuring that our application process provides an equal employment opportunity to all job seekers, including individuals from diverse gender, cultural and linguistic backgrounds, individuals with a disability, individuals identifying as being part of the LGBTQIA+ community, individuals who may have served in the armed forces or who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. We also want to do our best to make our recruitment process inclusive.

If you require any adjustments or accessibility support to participate fairly and equitably in the recruitment process, please email AccessibleRecruitment@optus.com.au or call 1800 309 170. We're here to help. When you contact us, please advise your preferred method of contact and we will be in touch within 48 hours.

For more information on Diversity, Inclusion & Belonging at Optus, please visit https://www.optus.com.au/about/inclusion-diversity

At Saputo, we bring good to the table by making high-quality products, investing in our people, and supporting communities around the world . As a top 10 global dairy processor, we value contributions that matter and strive to foster an inclusive, growth-driven work environment. Ready to bring your best?

About the Role

We’re recruiting a Senior Manager Commercial Finance – Retail to join our Domestic Commercial Finance leadership team at Saputo Dairy Australia. This is a senior commercial partnering role, working closely with the General Manager of Retail, with accountability for shaping commercial decisions across our largest retail customers. The role exists to strengthen forward‑looking insight and forecast accuracy, enabling clearer foresight, trajectory and value‑based decision‑making across the Retail business.

Reporting to the Head of Commercial Finance – Domestic, this role leads a team of Commercial Finance Business Partners and Analysts aligned to the Retail Sales structure. You will work closely with Retail leadership, Revenue Management, and cross‑functional stakeholders to ensure the business has the right plans and insights to support effective decision‑making. This is not a purely strategic or purely analytical role. It requires a leader who can operate confidently at the senior table, while also lifting capability, improving financial acumen, and establishing a strong cadence of commercial insight across the Retail business.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the financial planning, budgeting and forecasting for the Retail Sales function, with a strong focus on forecast accuracy and forward‑looking insight

Act as a trusted commercial partner to the General Manager of Retail, influencing decisions through clear analysis, insight and commercial judgement

Lead, develop and uplift a team of Commercial Finance Business Partners and Analysts, embedding strong business partnering capability

Partner closely with Revenue Management on BAU pricing, and own the translation of pricing and commercial initiatives into clear P&L and performance impacts

Establish strong cadence and commercial storytelling with Retail leadership, providing insight into trends, risks, opportunities and future trajectories

About You

Extensive experience (typically 10+ years) in commercial finance, business partnering or performance‑focused roles within FMCG or complex commercial environments

CA / CPA qualified, with strong commercial acumen and financial judgement

Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders and operate as a trusted thought partner

Demonstrated experience leading and developing teams with varying capability levels

Comfortable balancing strategic leadership with hands‑on insight, problem solving and execution

Strong communicator, able to translate complex financial information into clear, actionable insight

What We Offer

A senior commercial finance leadership role with direct influence on Retail performance

Exposure to large‑scale customer portfolios and complex commercial decision‑making

Opportunity to shape how commercial finance partners the Retail business in a clearly defined end‑state structure

Strong visibility across senior commercial and finance leadership




Click “Apply for this job” to submit your application. Applicants must have full Australian working rights.




As a leading dairy processor in Australia, Saputo produces, markets, and distributes a variety of products, with a portfolio of well-loved brands such as Devondale, Liddells, CHEER, Mersey Valley and Tasmanian Heritage. Our people bring these popular brands to life, while servicing our farming communities and through our AG Warehouse network.

Whether your expertise lies in manufacturing, operations, supply chain, sales, quality assurance, or in any other function, your role is integral to our success. You will make contributions that matter, all while working alongside colleagues who genuinely care about your success and who will roll up their sleeves to help.

Our client is an iconic multinational consumer goods organisation that is continuing to invest in its growth and has capitalised on an increasing amount of market share within its sector. Working within a world-class finance division presents an excellent opportunity for an ambitious Group FP&A Manager to join the team.

The role is accountable for the end‑to‑end leadership, governance and delivery of Group‑wide financial planning, forecasting and performance reporting, ensuring consistency, integrity and clarity of financial outputs across all regions.

The Organisation

Private equity owned, this diversified business has experienced significant organic and acquisition related growth. It is now entering a critical phase of transformation—reshaping how it operates, scales and delivers value to customers.

With a clear mandate and strategic focus, they are redesigning their operating model to unlock performance, improve accountability, and enable faster, more aligned decision-making across the enterprise to drive sustainable growth.

The Opportunity

This is a rare opportunity to lead the design and rollout of a new enterprise-wide operating model.

Reporting into both the Chief Growth & Transformation Officer and Chief People Officer, you will sit at the centre of a complex transformation, partnering with the CEO, CFO and senior leadership team to define how the organisation is structured, governed and run.

You will lead the end-to-end design and implementation of the operating model—spanning structure, roles, decision rights, governance, processes and systems ownership, ensuring it is not only well designed, but fully embedded.

Key accountabilities: 
Operating Model Design – Lead the detailed design of the future-state operating model, including structure, roles, governance, decision rights and process ownership
Stakeholder Alignment – Partner with the CEO, C-suite and senior leaders to align on key design decisions, trade-offs and priorities
Transformation Workstream Leadership – Establish and lead cross-functional workstreams, ensuring clarity of scope, pace and delivery across the program
Governance & Decision-Making – Design and embed governance forums, decision-making frameworks, RACI models and escalation pathways
Industrial & People Impact – Navigate organisational and industrial implications, ensuring changes are implemented transparently and responsibly
Implementation & Rollout – Develop and execute a phased rollout plan, balancing urgency with organisational readiness
Change Integration – Work closely with transformation and change teams to ensure the model is adopted and sustained
Benefits Realisation – Define and track success metrics, ensuring the new model delivers improved efficiency, clarity and performance

The Right Fit

You are an experienced transformation leader who has designed effective organisation structures in complex manufacturing, supply chain, operations and or retail environments across diverse global portfolios. 

You bring both structure and pragmatism, and you know how to navigate complexity to deliver outcomes at pace. Key to success will be leveraging the following capability: 
Proven experience designing and implementing enterprise operating models in complex organisations as part of people and culture or enterprise transformation programs;
Strong stakeholder management capability, with experience influencing executive and board-level leaders;
Deep understanding of governance, decision rights, organisational design and process ownership;
Experience leading cross-functional transformation program streams that deliver complex outcomes at pace;
The ability to deliver value and momentum with consideration of risk, including industrial or workforce implications;
Strategic and hands-on delivery that embeds change and new ways of working effectively to lift performance and culture. 

Why This Role Matters

This is a unique opportunity to make you mark on an organisation and support a key growth agenda. If successful, you will: 
Shape how the organisation operates for years to come;
Work directly with the executive team on decisions that materially impact performance;
Bring clarity to complexity—reducing duplication, accelerating decision-making and improving accountability;
Play a central role in a broader transformation agenda that has an enterprise-critical mandate.
